classObjective: Sketch graphs of real-world functions from verbal descriptions, identifying intercepts, intervals of increase and decrease, end behavior, extrema, and symmetry.
workedExamples: 3-6, Example 1 — Sketch Linear Functions from Verbal Descriptions
3-6, Example 2 — Sketch Nonlinear Functions and Interpret Key Features
3-6, Example 3 — Sketch and Interpret Real-World Data Trends
warmUp: What key features should you identify when sketching a function from a verbal description?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: linear sketching from descriptions; parabolic trajectories; data trend interpretation
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice sketching graphs of real-world functions from verbal descriptions with all key features
independentPractice: Practice problems sketching function graphs from verbal descriptions including intercepts, behavior, extrema, and symmetry
assessment: Exit ticket: sketch a function described verbally and identify its key features
capReflection: What is the minimum information you need from a verbal description to sketch an accurate graph?
